questions.text: Which feature starts a new line whenever a word or sentence reached a border?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Word Art is a feature in word processing software that allows you to create decorative text with special effects. It does not control how text lines break.
Gutter margin refers to the space between the edge of the page and the main body of text. It does not control how text lines break within the main body of text
Strikethrough is a formatting style that adds a line through the text. It does not control how text lines break

Text Wrapping is the correct answer. Text wrapping is a feature in word processing software that automatically moves text to the next line when it reaches the edge of the margin. This ensures that the text stays within the defined margins and does not overflow onto the next page unintentionally.

questions.explanation: Text Wrapping is the correct answer. Text wrapping is a feature in word processing software that automatically moves text to the next line when it reaches the edge of the margin. This ensures that the text stays within the defined margins and does not overflow onto the next page unintentionally.

questions.text: In which typing mode does the newly entered text replace the old text
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Insertion Mode (default mode) is used when newly entered text is inserted at the cursor location, pushing existing text to the right
Overtype Mode replaces existing characters with the new characters you type as you type over them
Editing Mode is not a specific typing mode. Editing typically refers to modifying existing text, which can involve inserting, deleting, or replacing text
Web Layout is a view mode in some word processing software that allows you to see how your document will look when viewed in a web browser. It is not a typing mode

questions.explanation: Overtype Mode replaces existing characters with the new characters you type as you type over them

questions.text: The distance of each line of paragraph to the margin is called
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Line spacing (Option A) refers to the vertical distance between lines of text. It is separate from indentation, which refers to the horizontal distance from the margin
Margins (Option B) refers to the entire outer edges of the page. While indentation is a distance from the margins, it is not the margins thems

Indentation refers to the space between the text and the margin. It creates a visual separation between the paragraph and the margin, making the text easier to read. You can adjust the indentation to control how far the text is inset from the margin

Alignment (Option D) refers to how the lines of text are positioned within the margins. For instance, text can be left-aligned, right-aligned, or justified. Alignment does not refer specifically to the distance from the margin

questions.explanation: Indentation refers to the space between the text and the margin. It creates a visual separation between the paragraph and the margin, making the text easier to read. You can adjust the indentation to control how far the text is inset from the margin

questions.text: Which of the following features is used to connect an MS Word document with a webpage?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Watermark is an image or text that is placed behind the content of a document and typically appears faintly. It does not link to webpages
Header & Footer are areas at the top and bottom of each page in a document where you can insert text or images that appear consistently on every page. They are not used to create links

Hyperlink is the correct answer. A hyperlink is an element within a document that, when clicked, directs you to a different location. In MS Word, hyperlinks can be inserted into text or images and can link to webpages, other documents, email addresses, or other locations
Insert page number automatically inserts page numbers into your document. It does not create links

questions.explanation: Hyperlink is the correct answer. A hyperlink is an element within a document that, when clicked, directs you to a different location. In MS Word, hyperlinks can be inserted into text or images and can link to webpages, other documents, email addresses, or other locations

questions.text: Which display the name of the active application in Microsoft Word?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: The Title bar located at the top of the window displays the name of the application and the currently open document. In this case, it shows "Microsoft Word - Document1".
The Menu bar located below the title bar contains menus with various options for formatting, inserting, and editing text in your document. It does not display the application name
The Standard toolbar located below the menu bar contains buttons for commonly used functions like saving, copying, pasting, and formatting text. It also does not display the application name
The Ruler is a horizontal or vertical bar that helps measure and align text and objects in your document. It does not display the application name

questions.explanation: The Title bar located at the top of the window displays the name of the application and the currently open document. In this case, it shows "Microsoft Word - Document1".

questions.text: Which dialog box is opened by pressing F12 key
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Open dialog box (Option A) is typically opened using the Ctrl+O keyboard shortcut or by clicking the "File" menu and selecting "Open"
Box dialog box (Option B) is not a standard term used in Microsoft Office applications

Pressing F12 in most Microsoft Office applications, including Word, Excel, and PowerPoint, opens the Save As dialog box. This dialog box allows you to specify a filename, location, and file format for saving a new document or saving an existing document with a different name or format

Pressing F12 in most Microsoft Office applications, including Word, Excel, and PowerPoint, opens the Save As dialog box. This dialog box allows you to specify a filename, location, and file format for saving a new document or saving an existing document with a different name or format

questions.explanation: Pressing F12 in most Microsoft Office applications, including Word, Excel, and PowerPoint, opens the Save As dialog box. This dialog box allows you to specify a filename, location, and file format for saving a new document or saving an existing document with a different name or format

questions.text: In MS Word document the font refers to the typeface of
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Fonts include letters of the alphabet.
Fonts can also include symbols such as punctuation marks, mathematical symbols, and currency symbols
Digits 0-9 are included in most fonts

A font in MS Word refers to the complete design and style of a set of characters, including letters, symbols, and digits. It encompasses characteristics like the typeface (design of the characters), size, weight (boldness), and slant (italics). While each option (letters, symbols, and digits) represents a specific type of character, a font applies to all the characters within a particular design style

questions.explanation: A font in MS Word refers to the complete design and style of a set of characters, including letters, symbols, and digits. It encompasses characteristics like the typeface (design of the characters), size, weight (boldness), and slant (italics). While each option (letters, symbols, and digits) represents a specific type of character, a font applies to all the characters within a particular design style

questions.text: Which option is used to move the cursor to the next line automatically during typing?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Word wrap is a feature that automatically moves the cursor to the next line when you reach the end of a line of text. This prevents you from having to press the Enter key to move to a new line. Word wrap is enabled by default in most word processing software programs
Search and Replace is a feature used to find specific text in your document and replace it with other text. It does not affect how the cursor moves as you type
Cut and Paste is a feature used to remove text or graphics from one location in your document and insert it in another location. It does not affect how the cursor moves as you type
Page size and Margin are settings that control the size of the printable area on a page and the amount of space around the edges of the page. They do not affect how the cursor moves as you type

questions.explanation: Word wrap is a feature that automatically moves the cursor to the next line when you reach the end of a line of text. This prevents you from having to press the Enter key to move to a new line. Word wrap is enabled by default in most word processing software programs

questions.text: Microsoft Word is which type of software
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: An application program is a software program designed to perform a specific task for the user. Microsoft Word is a word processor application that allows users to create, edit, format, and print documents
Compiler/Translator language is a program that translates code written in a programming language into a machine code that the computer can understand. Microsoft Word is not used for this purpose
System Software is software that manages the computer hardware and provides a platform for running other applications. Microsoft Word is an application that runs on top of the operating system
Programming Language is a language used to write instructions for computers. Microsoft Word is not a programming language

questions.explanation: An application program is a software program designed to perform a specific task for the user. Microsoft Word is a word processor application that allows users to create, edit, format, and print documents

questions.text: Which shortcut key do you press to force a page break in Word
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Not standard shortcut keys for inserting a page break in Word
Ctrl + Break (Option B) is a keyboard shortcut that can be used to interrupt or pause a process in some programs, but it is not used for inserting page breaks in Word

Pressing Ctrl + Enter together in Microsoft Word inserts a page break. A page break forces the text to start on a new page, regardless of how much space is left on the current page. This is useful when you want to ensure a new section of text, like a new chapter or topic, begins on a fresh page
Not standard shortcut keys for inserting a page break in Word

questions.explanation: Pressing Ctrl + Enter together in Microsoft Word inserts a page break. A page break forces the text to start on a new page, regardless of how much space is left on the current page. This is useful when you want to ensure a new section of text, like a new chapter or topic, begins on a fresh page

questions.text: Which of the following options is used to allow users to align text at a specific location in documents
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Tab Stops are specific positions on a line of text that you can set for the cursor to jump to when you press the Tab key. This allows you to align text at specific locations in your document, such as indenting paragraphs or creating columns of text

Alignment refers to the overall justification of text on a paragraph. Common alignment options include left-aligned, right-aligned, centered, and justified. While alignment can affect the position of text on a line, it does not allow for precise placement at specific locations like tab stops do
Line and paragraph spacing controls the amount of space between lines of text and between paragraphs. It does not directly affect the horizontal positioning of text on a line

Margins are the spaces around the edges of the page. They set the printable area of the document. While margins can indirectly affect the placement of text on a line, they are not used for precise alignment within a line

questions.explanation: Tab Stops are specific positions on a line of text that you can set for the cursor to jump to when you press the Tab key. This allows you to align text at specific locations in your document, such as indenting paragraphs or creating columns of text

questions.text: What character or word represents a series of keystrokes in MS Word
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Macro is the correct answer. A macro is a recorded series of instructions or keystrokes that can be played back to automate repetitive tasks in MS Word. Macros are created using the Macro Recorder feature in MS Word. They can be a powerful tool for saving time and effort when performing complex or frequently used sequences of steps

Mail Merge is a feature in MS Word that allows you to create personalized letters, emails, or other documents by merging data from a source file with a template document. It is a function of MS Word, but it does not represent a series of keystrokes
Spell Checker is a tool in MS Word that helps you identify and correct spelling errors in your document. It is a function of MS Word, but it does not represent a series of keystrokes
Watermark is an image or text that is placed behind the content of a document and typically appears faintly. It can be inserted into a document, but it does not represent a series of keystrokes

questions.explanation: Macro is the correct answer. A macro is a recorded series of instructions or keystrokes that can be played back to automate repetitive tasks in MS Word. Macros are created using the Macro Recorder feature in MS Word. They can be a powerful tool for saving time and effort when performing complex or frequently used sequences of steps

questions.text: The blinking vertical bar in the MS-Word document is called
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: The blinking vertical bar in the MS-Word document is called an insertion point. It indicates where the next typed character will appear in the document
Paragraph (Option B) refers to a section of text that typically starts on a new line and ends with a pressing Enter key. The paragraph itself is not a marker or indicator
I-beam (Option C) is another term sometimes used to refer to the insertion point because of its resemblance to the capital letter "I"

End mark (Option D) refers to punctuation marks that signal the end of a sentence or paragraph. The insertion point does not indicate the end of anything, but rather the beginning of where the next typed character will be placed

questions.explanation: The blinking vertical bar in the MS-Word document is called an insertion point. It indicates where the next typed character will appear in the document

questions.text: Which option is used to change the case of text?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Shift + F3 is a keyboard shortcut that cycles through different text case options in Microsoft Word. Pressing it repeatedly will change the selected text between lowercase, uppercase, and Title Case (where the first letter of each word is uppercase).
Ctrl + F3 is not a shortcut for changing text case in Microsoft Word
Alt + F3 is not a shortcut for changing text case in Microsoft Word
Ctrl + Shift + F3 is not a shortcut for changing text case in Microsoft Word

questions.explanation: Shift + F3 is a keyboard shortcut that cycles through different text case options in Microsoft Word. Pressing it repeatedly will change the selected text between lowercase, uppercase, and Title Case (where the first letter of each word is uppercase).

questions.text: Which option is used to reverse the undo action in MS Word
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Undo is the action of reversing the last change you made to a document. It would not be used to reverse the undo action itself
Redo is the correct answer. After you undo an action in MS Word, the redo function allows you to bring back the changes you just undid
Cut is used to remove and store selected content to the clipboard. It is not used to undo or redo actions
Reverse is not a standard function within MS Word for undoing or redoing actions.

questions.explanation: Redo is the correct answer. After you undo an action in MS Word, the redo function allows you to bring back the changes you just undid

questions.text: Which feature of Word allows you to embed illustrations, pictures, and images to a document
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Graphics is not a specific feature in MS Word for inserting images. While graphics can refer to visual elements in general, Word uses a more specific term for inserting images.
Font Specification defines the appearance of text in a document, such as font style, size, and color. It does not refer to inserting images
Footnotes/Cross-references are used to add explanatory notes or references to other parts of the document. They are not used for inserting images.
Picture is the correct answer. In MS Word, the "Picture" function within the "Insert" tab allows you to insert images from your computer files or online sources into your document

questions.explanation: Picture is the correct answer. In MS Word, the "Picture" function within the "Insert" tab allows you to insert images from your computer files or online sources into your document

questions.text: Which shortcut key is used to check spelling and grammar in MS Word
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Shift + F7 is not a shortcut for spelling and grammar check in MS Word
Ctrl + F7 is not a shortcut for spelling and grammar check in MS Word
Alt + F7 opens the Thesaurus pane in MS Word, which allows you to find synonyms for selected words. It does not check spelling or grammar

F7 is the keyboard shortcut to start a spelling and grammar check in MS Word. When you press F7, Word will highlight the first potential spelling or grammar error and offer suggestions for correction in a pane that appears next to the error

questions.explanation: F7 is the keyboard shortcut to start a spelling and grammar check in MS Word. When you press F7, Word will highlight the first potential spelling or grammar error and offer suggestions for correction in a pane that appears next to the error

questions.text: Which of the following software is used for making resumes?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: MS Excel (Option A) is a spreadsheet application primarily designed for numerical data and calculations. While it can be used to create basic tables, it's not ideal for formatting text and layouts required in a professional resume

MS Word is a widely used word processing application that offers features and functionalities well-suited for creating resumes. It provides resume templates, formatting options for text and layout, and allows for easy integration of graphics or images
are programming languages. While they can be used to create software applications, they are not typically used for creating resumes.
are programming languages. While they can be used to create software applications, they are not typically used for creating resumes.

questions.explanation: MS Word is a widely used word processing application that offers features and functionalities well-suited for creating resumes. It provides resume templates, formatting options for text and layout, and allows for easy integration of graphics or images

questions.text: Which short key is used to open the window Help in MS Word document
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: F1 is the universal shortcut key to access the Help feature in most Windows applications, including MS Word. Pressing F1 will open the Help window and display context-sensitive help based on the current task you are performing in Word
F2 is a shortcut key used for renaming files and folders in Windows. It does not open the Help window in Word
F3 is a shortcut key used for various functions depending on the context in Word. For example, it can be used to search for text or repeat the last formatting action. It does not open the Help window
F11 is the shortcut key used to toggle between the full screen mode and the normal window mode in MS Word. It does not open the Help window.

questions.explanation: F1 is the universal shortcut key to access the Help feature in most Windows applications, including MS Word. Pressing F1 will open the Help window and display context-sensitive help based on the current task you are performing in Word

questions.text: Which of the following software is used for making resume
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: MS Excel is a spreadsheet application designed for working with data in a grid of rows and columns. While it can be used to create basic tables for resumes, it is not typically the preferred software for creating resumes due to its lack of layout and formatting features specifically designed for resumes

MS Word is the most widely used software for creating resumes. It offers a variety of features that are helpful for resume creation, such as templates, formatting options, and spell checking. MS Word allows you to easily format text, add headers and footers, and insert tables and images to create a professional-looking resume. While there are other word processing applications and online resume builders available, MS Word remains a popular choice due to its familiarity and wide range of features

are programming languages. While it is possible to programmatically generate a resume using a scripting language, this is not a typical approach for resume creation. Resumes are typically created using word processing software that offers features for formatting text, inserting images, and creating layouts

are programming languages. While it is possible to programmatically generate a resume using a scripting language, this is not a typical approach for resume creation. Resumes are typically created using word processing software that offers features for formatting text, inserting images, and creating layouts

questions.explanation: MS Word is the most widely used software for creating resumes. MS Word allows you to easily format text, add headers and footers, and insert tables and images to create a professional-looking resume. questions.text: Which of the following options shows positioning of text, tabs, margins and another element on the page
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Rulers in word processing software visually represent the margins, tabs, and indentations set for the document. They allow you to see the positioning of text and other elements relative to the page boundaries.
typically displays information about the current state of the document, such as the page number, section number, or editing mode. It doesn't directly show positioning details

represents the entire editable area where you see and interact with your document content. While it indirectly shows text and element placement, it lacks the specific visual cues for margins, tabs, and indentation offered by rulers
provides buttons and icons for quick access to various formatting and editing functions. It doesn't directly display document layout or element positioning.

questions.explanation: Rulers in word processing software visually represent the margins, tabs, and indentations set for the document. They allow you to see the positioning of text and other elements relative to the page boundaries.

questions.text: Which bar shows the current position as far as the text goes
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Title bar typically displays the name of the document or program you are working on
Menu bar provides access to the various menus and functions of the program
Scroll bar allows you to scroll up and down through the document to view different sections. It does not indicate the current position within the text itself

Status bar often displays information about the current document, such as the page number, line number, and column number. In the context of your question, the status bar is the most likely candidate to show the current position as far as the text goes, as it can display the line number or cursor position. While the specific content displayed in the status bar may vary depending on the software program, it generally provides useful information about the current state of the document or program

questions.explanation: Status bar often displays information about the current document, such as the page number, line number, and column number. In the context of your question, the status bar is the most likely candidate to show the current position as far as the text goes, as it can display the line number or cursor position. While the specific content displayed in the status bar may vary depending on the software program, it generally provides useful information about the current state of the document or program

questions.text: Which of the following can be used to launch the Word Art option in MS Word
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Status bar located at the bottom of the window displays information about the current document, such as the page number, section number, and typing mode. It does not contain options for formatting text or inserting objects like WordArt

Drawing toolbar contains buttons for creating and formatting graphical objects, such as lines, shapes, and clip art. WordArt is a feature that allows you to add text with special effects like shadows, bevels, and gradients. It is typically found on the Drawing toolbar
Standard toolbar contains buttons for commonly used functions like saving, copying, pasting, and formatting text. It does not contain the WordArt option
Ruler is a horizontal or vertical bar that helps measure and align text and objects in your document. It does not contain buttons for inserting objects like WordArt

questions.explanation: Drawing toolbar contains buttons for creating and formatting graphical objects, such as lines, shapes, and clip art. WordArt is a feature that allows you to add text with special effects like shadows, bevels, and gradients. It is typically found on the Drawing toolbar

questions.text: Which of the following features of MS Word enables us to send the same letter to different persons
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Macros (Option A) can automate repetitive tasks in Word, but they are not specifically designed for sending personalized letters
﻿Template/Layout (Option B) can provide a starting point for formatting and styling a letter, but it wouldn't include functionality for personalizing content for multiple recipients

Mail Merge is the feature in MS Word that enables you to send the same form letter to different recipients by merging a document with a data source. A data source is typically a list containing recipient information like names, addresses, or other details
Hyperlink (Option D) creates links within a document or to external web pages. It's not relevant to sending personalized letters

questions.explanation: Mail Merge is the feature in MS Word that enables you to send the same form letter to different recipients by merging a document with a data source. A data source is typically a list containing recipient information like names, addresses, or other details

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of a simple word processor?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Wordpad is a basic word processor that comes bundled with Microsoft Windows. It allows you to create and edit text documents, but it offers fewer features than more advanced word processors like Microsoft Word. However, it provides more functionality than Notepad for creating formatted documents
Notepad is a simple text editor that is also included with Windows. It allows you to create and edit plain text files. However, it does not support features like formatting, fonts

PowerPoint is a presentation software application developed by Microsoft. It is used to create slideshow presentations that can include text, images, videos, and other multimedia elements. It is not a word processor and is not designed for creating and editing text documents

While Wordpad offers more features than Notepad, both are considered simple word processors because they lack the advanced features found in more sophisticated word processing software like Microsoft Word. These simpler programs are useful for creating basic text documents or taking notes, but they may not be suitable for complex formatting or desktop publishing tasks

questions.explanation: While Wordpad offers more features than Notepad, both are considered simple word processors because they lack the advanced features found in more sophisticated word processing software like Microsoft Word. These simpler programs are useful for creating basic text documents or taking notes, but they may not be suitable for complex formatting or desktop publishing tasks

questions.text: Which of the following shortcut keys is used to select all the text in a document?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Ctrl + T is a shortcut key used to open a new tab in many web browsers or file explorer windows. It is not used to select text in a document
Ctrl + B is a shortcut key used to bold selected text in many word processing programs, including MS Word. It is not used to select all the text
Ctrl + A is the most widely used shortcut key to select all the text in a document or text field across different applications and operating systems
Ctrl + V is a shortcut key used to paste previously copied text at the cursor location in many applications. It is not used to select text

questions.explanation: Ctrl + A is the most widely used shortcut key to select all the text in a document or text field across different applications and operating systems

questions.text: Footnotes, endnotes, and indexes are all inserted as
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Bookmarks (Option A) are used to mark specific locations in a document for easy reference. They are not specifically designed for linking footnotes, endnotes, or indexes

Cross-references are used to link different parts of a document. In the case of footnotes, endnotes, and indexes, cross-references create links between the place in the text where the reference is mentioned (e.g., with a superscript number) and the corresponding footnote, endnote, or index entry. Clicking or following the cross-reference will take you to the relevant footnote, endnote, or index entry, and vice versa
Hyperlinks (Option C) can create links to web pages or other electronic documents, but they are not the typical way to link footnotes, endnotes, or indexes within a document

Word fields (Option D) are used to insert dynamic content into a document, such as dates, page numbers, or custom information. While they can be used in footnotes and endnotes, they are not the primary way to create the links between the reference in the text and the corresponding footnote or endnote

questions.explanation: Cross-references are used to link different parts of a document. In the case of footnotes, endnotes, and indexes, cross-references create links between the place in the text where the reference is mentioned (e.g., with a superscript number) and the corresponding footnote, endnote, or index entry. Clicking or following the cross-reference will take you to the relevant footnote, endnote, or index entry, and vice versa

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of a full-featured word processor
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: MS-Word is a full-featured word processor developed by Microsoft. It is widely used for creating and editing professional documents, letters, reports, and more. It offers a wide range of features including:• Text formatting options (font styles, sizes, colors)• Paragraph formatting (alignment, indentation, spacing)• Spell checking and grammar checking• Styles and templates for consistent formatting• Tables and charts• Headers and footers• Mail merge for creating personalized letters• Collaboration features for working on documents with othersMS Word is a full-featured word processor that offers a comprehensive set of tools for creating, editing, formatting, and printing professional documents. It is a versatile tool that can be used for a wide range of tasks

MS-Excel is a spreadsheet application also developed by Microsoft. It is used for creating and manipulating data in a grid of rows and columns. While it can be used for basic text editing, it is not designed for the comprehensive document creation features offered by a word processor

Power Point is a presentation software application also developed by Microsoft. It is used for creating slideshow presentations that can include text, images, videos, and other multimedia elements. It is not a word processor and is not designed for creating and editing text documents

Data Base is a category of software applications that are used to store, organize, and manage data. There are many different database management systems available, but none of them are considered word processors. They are designed for data management tasks, not document creation.

questions.explanation: MS-Word is a full-featured word processor developed by Microsoft. It is widely used for creating and editing professional documents, letters, reports, and more. It offers a wide range of features including:• Text formatting options (font styles, sizes, colors)• Paragraph formatting (alignment, indentation, spacing)• Spell checking and grammar checking• Styles and templates for consistent formatting• Tables and charts• Headers and footers• Mail merge for creating personalized letters• Collaboration features for working on documents with othersMS Word is a full-featured word processor that offers a comprehensive set of tools for creating, editing, formatting, and printing professional documents. It is a versatile tool that can be used for a wide range of tasks

questions.text: Which of the following is used to count the word in a MS Word document
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Clipboard is a temporary storage area that holds copied or cut data. It does not have a function to count words in a document
Alignment refers to how text is positioned on a line or paragraph. It does not have a function to count words in a document
Drop cap is a formatting style where the first letter of a paragraph is enlarged and drops below the first line of text. It does not have a function to count words in a document

Word count is a feature in MS Word that displays various statistics about the document, including the number of words, characters, pages, paragraphs, and lines. It is typically accessed from the Review tab

questions.explanation: Word count is a feature in MS Word that displays various statistics about the document, including the number of words, characters, pages, paragraphs, and lines. It is typically accessed from the Review tab

questions.text: Which defines the boundaries of the page in MS Word
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Margins define the boundaries of the printable area on a page in MS Word. They set the space between the edge of the page and the content of your document. You can adjust margins to control how much space is left around the edges of your document In conclusion, margins are the primary way to establish the printable area on a page in MS Word

Indents (Option B) refer to the extra space added to the left or right margins of a paragraph or a block of text. While they can affect how close text appears to the edge of the page, they don't define the overall printable area.
Line spacing (Option C) controls the vertical distance between lines of text in a paragraph. It doesn't influence the page boundaries
Paragraph spacing (Option D) refers to the space added before or after paragraphs. Similar to line spacing, it doesn't define the printable area of the page

questions.explanation: Margins define the boundaries of the printable area on a page in MS Word. They set the space between the edge of the page and the content of your document. You can adjust margins to control how much space is left around the edges of your document In conclusion, margins are the primary way to establish the printable area on a page in MS Word

questions.text: Which shortcut key is used to print a MS-Word document
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Ctrl+N is a shortcut key used to create a new document in many applications, including MS Word. It is not used to print a document.
Ctrl+X is a shortcut key used to cut selected text or objects in many applications. It is not used to print a document

Ctrl+P is the most widely used shortcut key to initiate printing across different applications and operating systems. In MS Word, it opens the print dialog box where you can specify printing options such as the number of copies, printer selection, and page range
Ctrl+A is a shortcut key used to select all the text in a document or text field across different applications and operating systems. It is not used to print a document

questions.explanation: Ctrl+P is the most widely used shortcut key to initiate printing across different applications and operating systems. In MS Word, it opens the print dialog box where you can specify printing options such as the number of copies, printer selection, and page range

questions.text: Which of the following is a temporarily holding space in the computer memory for data that is being copied or moved
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Clip Board (Option A) is a temporary storage area on the clipboard that holds data or content you've copied or cut. It's not directly involved in data transfer within the computer's memory

Printing Document (Option B) refers to a document that is spooled (staged) for printing. While data might be temporarily stored during spooling, it's not a general purpose buffer for data transfers within memory

A buffer is a temporary storage area in computer memory that holds data during transfer between different parts of a computer system. It acts as an intermediary, reducing the waiting time between a slower and faster device. For instance, when you copy data from one location to another, the data is first placed in a buffer before being written to the destination In conclusion, a buffer is the most suitable answer for a temporarily holding space in computer memory for data transfer
Word Wrap (Option D) is a feature in word processing software that automatically moves text to the next line when it reaches the end of the margin. It doesn't involve temporary storage of data

questions.explanation: A buffer is a temporary storage area in computer memory that holds data during transfer between different parts of a computer system. It acts as an intermediary, reducing the waiting time between a slower and faster device. For instance, when you copy data from one location to another, the data is first placed in a buffer before being written to the destination In conclusion, a buffer is the most suitable answer for a temporarily holding space in computer memory for data transfer

questions.text: Which of the following dimension is used by legal size page

options.explanation: 8.5" x 11" is the standard size for letter paper in the United States and Canada. It is not the legal size

8.5" x 15" is not a common standard paper size

questions.text: Which bar is usually located below the title bar?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: The menu bar is typically located below the title bar in graphical user interfaces (GUIs). It provides a list of menus that contain options and commands for interacting with the program. Clicking on a menu item will reveal a dropdown list of further options or actions

Toolbar (Option C) can be found below the menu bar or elsewhere in the window and provides buttons or icons for frequently used functions
Scrollbar (Option D) appears on the side or bottom of the window when the content extends beyond the visible area and allows you to scroll through the content.

questions.text: Header and footer option can be used from which menu
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Tools menu in older versions of Microsoft Word contained various options for customizing the overall look and feel of the document, but the header and footer options were typically located elsewhere

Insert menu is the most common location for header and footer options in Microsoft Word. It allows you to insert pre-designed headers and footers or create your own custom headers and footers that can include text, images, page numbers, and other elements
View menu controls how you view the document on the screen. It does not contain options for inserting headers and footers
Edit menu contains options for editing text and formatting the document. It does not contain options for inserting headers and footers

options.explanation: Standard toolbar typically contains buttons for common actions like saving, opening, printing, and copying. It may not contain options for font formatting

Formatting toolbar is specifically designed for formatting text, including changing fonts and their sizes. It typically includes buttons or menus for selecting fonts, font sizes, bold, italics, underline, and other text formatting options
Print Preview toolbar is used to preview how a document will look when printed. It does not allow editing of the document content or formatting

questions.text: What is the part of the font called that has curves or extra decorative lines at the end of the strokes
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Serif (Option A) refers to the small lines or extensions that are added to the ends of strokes in some fonts. While serifs can add a decorative element, they are not the same as swashes, which are typically more elaborate and flourish-like
Baseline (Option B) is the imaginary line upon which most letters sit in a font. It does not refer to decorative elements

X-height (Option C) is the height of the lowercase letter "x" in a font, excluding ascenders (parts that extend above the x-height) and descenders (parts that extend below the baseline). It is a measurement used to compare the relative size of lowercase letters in different fonts

Swash is the part of the font that has curves or extra decorative lines at the end of the strokes. Swashes can add a decorative or flamboyant touch to the font and are often used in display fonts or headings

questions.explanation: Swash is the part of the font that has curves or extra decorative lines at the end of the strokes. Swashes can add a decorative or flamboyant touch to the font and are often used in display fonts or headings

questions.text: Which feature of MS Word helps to reduce spelling errors in the document?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: AutoFormat is a feature in MS Word that automatically applies formatting styles to text as you type. For example, it might automatically change plain text to a heading style or bulleted list. While it can improve the look of your document, it doesn't directly address spelling errors

AutoCorrect is a feature in MS Word that automatically corrects common typos and spelling mistakes as you type. For instance, it can change "teh" to "the" or "maicrosoft" to "Microsoft". This helps to reduce errors in your document In conclusion, AutoCorrect is the feature that helps reduce spelling errors in a Word document by automatically correcting common mistakes as you type

Smart Tags are small informational icons that appear when you type certain things in MS Word. For example, if you type a date, a Smart Tag might appear that allows you to convert it to a calendar event. Smart Tags are not related to spell checking
AutoText is a feature in MS Word that allows you to insert pre-defined blocks of text or symbols with a shortcut. While it can save time typing, it doesn't address spelling errors

questions.text: What is the feature of Microsoft Word represented by the red box
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: The ribbon (Option A) is the graphical user interface element highlighted in red in the image. It replaced the traditional pull-down menus and toolbars in Microsoft Word 2007 and later versions. The ribbon organizes commands and features into tabs and groups, providing a visually accessible way to find and use the functionalities in Word. In conclusion, the ribbon is the most prominent element in the image and represents the core user interface for controlling features and functionalities in Microsoft Word

Status bar (Option B) is typically located at the bottom of the window and displays information about the current state of the document, such as the page number or editing mode. It is not the element highlighted in red

Ruler (Option C) is a horizontal or vertical bar that displays markings to help measure and align text and objects in the document. It is usually located above the document editing area and is not the highlighted element
Zoom slider (Option D) is a tool that allows you to adjust the magnification level of the document view. While it might be present near the bottom of the window, it's not the ribbon structure itself

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a font style
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Superscript is a character formatting option that positions the character slightly above the baseline of the text. It is typically used for mathematical expressions, scientific notation, or citations. Superscript is not a font style itself, but rather a way of positioning a character relative to the baseline

questions.explanation: Bold, Italics, and Regular are all different font styles. These styles change the appearance of the typeface but keep the characters on the same baseline. questions.text: What is the default number of lines to drop for the drop-cap feature of MS Word

options.explanation: The default number of lines to drop for the drop-cap feature in MS Word is 3. This means that the first letter of your paragraph will be enlarged and occupy the space of three text lines

questions.text: Which of the following are related to page orientation in MS-Word

options.explanation: Landscape refers to a wider orientation where the width is larger than the height, typically used for spreadsheets or wide tables
Portrait refers to a taller orientation where the height is larger than the width, commonly used for documents and letters

Legal is a standard paper size (8.5 inches by 14 inches) used in legal documents in some countries, but it is not a page orientation itself. You can set the page orientation to either portrait or landscape while using the Legal paper size

Both Landscape and Portrait are valid page orientations in MS Word

questions.text: How many types of alignments are there in MS Word
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: There are four types of alignments in MS Word:• Left-aligned: The text is aligned along the left margin of the document. This is the most common type of alignment.• Right-aligned: The text is aligned along the right margin of the document.• Center-aligned: The text is centered between the left and right margins of the document.• Justified: The text is aligned both along the left and right margins of the document. This is achieved by adding extra space between words or letters as needed to create straight margins on both sides.The alignment buttons are typically found in the Paragraph group on the Home tab in MS Word. They allow you to quickly set the alignment for your text

questions.text: Which key is used to delete the character to the left of the cursor

questions.text: Which of the following features belongs to font weight

options.explanation: Italic slants the characters to one side, typically right-leaning. It is a font style that affects the way characters look but maintains the baseline
Underline adds a line beneath the characters. It is a formatting option that doesn't affect the weight or thickness of the font itself

Bold increases the thickness of the font, making it appear darker and heavier. This is a property of font weight In conclusion, bold is the only option that belongs to font weight. Font weight refers to the variations in thickness of the strokes that make up the characters in a font. Bold is a common weight option, but other weights might be available depending on the specific font

questions.text: What is the symbol used to denote a bulleted list in MS Word
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: While some pre-defined bullet styles may include circles, asterisks are the standard bullet point symbol

An asterisk (*) is the most common symbol used to denote a bulleted list in MS Word. When you press the asterisk key and then start typing, Word automatically creates a bulleted list. You can also use other symbols or formatting options to create custom bullet styles. Circle (Option A), Square (Option C), and Dash (Option D) can also be used to create bulleted lists in Word, but they are not the default symbol. You would need to select a different bullet style from the menu to use these symbols

questions.text: Which shortcut key is used to search and replace in MS Word?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Ctrl + H is the correct answer. This is the keyboard shortcut for opening the Find and Replace dialog box in MS Word. This dialog box allows you to search for specific text or formatting within your document and replace it with something else.

questions.text: Which of the following features of MS Word includes formatting text
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: While adding pictures and graphics can enhance the visual appeal of your document, it is not considered formatting text in the strict sense. Formatting text refers to changing the appearance of the text itself, such as font style, size, and color
Controlling layouts refers to arranging elements on a page, such as text, pictures, and tables. While formatting text can be part of controlling the layout, it is a distinct concept

Formatting text in MS Word encompasses a wide range of features that allow you to modify the appearance of your text. Some of the common formatting options include:• Font: This refers to the typeface of the text, such as Times New Roman or Arial.• Font size: This refers to the size of the text, measured in points.• Bold, italic, underline, and other styles: These options can be used to add emphasis or distinction to your text.• Font color: You can change the color of your text to make it stand out or match a specific color scheme.• Alignment: You can align text to the left, right, or center of the page.• Paragraph formatting: This includes options like indentation, spacing, and line breaks.By using these formatting features effectively, you can create professional-looking documents that are easy to read and navigate

questions.text: Which tool will allow you to draw many predefined geometrical shapes, arrows, flowchart symbols and stars
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: This is the correct answer. AutoShapes is a toolbar in drawing programs like Microsoft Word, PowerPoint, and Excel that allows you to insert a variety of pre-defined shapes, including geometric shapes, arrows, flowchart symbols, and stars

WordArt is a feature in drawing programs that allows you to add text with special effects, such as bevels, shadows, and gradients. While you can use WordArt to create some shapes with text, it is not designed for drawing the wide variety of shapes, arrows, and symbols that AutoShapes offers

Vector images are graphics that use mathematical formulas to define shapes and lines. This allows them to be scaled to any size without losing quality. While vector image editing software typically has tools for drawing shapes, these tools may not offer the same wide variety of pre-defined shapes and symbols as AutoShapes

Bitmaps are graphics that are made up of a grid of pixels. They are not well-suited for drawing shapes because they can become pixelated when scaled. While some bitmap editing software may have shape drawing tools, they generally do not offer the same functionality as AutoShapes

questions.explanation: This is the correct answer. AutoShapes is a toolbar in drawing programs like Microsoft Word, PowerPoint, and Excel that allows you to insert a variety of pre-defined shapes, including geometric shapes, arrows, flowchart symbols, and stars

questions.text: A painted picture of a MS-Word document is called
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: A painted picture of a Word document is a bitmap graphic because it is a digital representation of an analog image. Bitmap graphics are created by storing information about the color of each pixel in the image. This means that the quality of a bitmap graphic can be affected by its resolution. If you enlarge a bitmap graphic, the pixels will become more visible, and the image will start to look blurry. In conclusion, a painted picture of a MS-Word document is most accurately called a bitmap graphic

Vector graphics are images created using mathematical formulas that describe lines, shapes, and colors. They are resolution-independent, which means they can be scaled to any size without losing quality. A painted picture of a Word document would not be a vector graphic because it would be a representation of a real-world object, not a mathematical creation
WordArt is a feature in Microsoft Word that allows you to add decorative effects to text. It is not a type of image file
This option is incorrect because the other options are not all correct descriptions of a painted picture of a Word document

questions.explanation: A painted picture of a Word document is a bitmap graphic because it is a digital representation of an analog image. Bitmap graphics are created by storing information about the color of each pixel in the image. This means that the quality of a bitmap graphic can be affected by its resolution. If you enlarge a bitmap graphic, the pixels will become more visible, and the image will start to look blurry. In conclusion, a painted picture of a MS-Word document is most accurately called a bitmap graphic

Formatting refers to changing the appearance of text, such as font size, style, or color. It is not a feature for undoing or redoing actions.
This feature removes the selected text or object and places it on the clipboard. It does not undo or redo any actions.
This feature inserts the content from the clipboard into the document at the current cursor position. It does not undo or redo any actions

questions.text: Deleting a character to the left of the cursor is accomplished by passing the _ key
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: The Del key is typically used to delete the character to the right of the cursor, but in some programs, it can also be used to delete the character to the left of the cursor when used in combination with another key
is the most commonly used key for deleting characters to the left of the cursor, but based on the text in the image, it is not the correct answer in this case.
is used to move the cursor to the beginning of the line, not to delete characters
Ctrl Tab

questions.text: Which is a popular full-featured word processor?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: A spreadsheet is a computer application for organizing and manipulating data in rows and columns. A popular spreadsheet application is Microsoft Excel.
Word pad is a basic text editor that comes included with Microsoft Windows. It is more feature-rich than Notepad but not as powerful as a full-featured word processor like MS Word.

Notepad is a very basic text editor that comes included with Microsoft Windows. It allows you to create and edit plain text documents, but it does not have features like formatting, spell checking, or grammar checking

questions.text: Which of the following features enables you to reverse the changes you have made to a document?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: WYSIWYG stands for "What You See Is What You Get". It refers to a type of editor that displays the document on the screen exactly as it will appear when printed or saved. While WYSIWYG editors can be helpful for formatting documents, they are not specifically for undoing changes
he Redo function is used to reverse an Undo action. So, if you Undo a change and then decide you want it back, you can use Redo
This feature reverses the last action you performed in a document. It allows you to go back to the state the document was in before the last change.

Word wrap is a feature that automatically moves text to the next line when it reaches the end of the current line. This is helpful for preventing lines of text from running off the edge of the page, but it does not undo or redo changes

questions.text: What is the command used to move selected text from one document to another?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: The Replace function is used to find and replace specific text within a document, not to move text between documents.
Editing refers to any modification made to a document, and doesn't specify the action of moving text.
Formatting refers to changing the appearance of text, such as font size or style, not moving text between documents
Cutting removes the selected text from the current location and stores it in the clipboard. Pasting inserts the contents of the clipboard at the desired location in the new document

questions.text: What is the blinking vertical bar in the Word document window called?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: A paragraph is a section of text that typically deals with a single point or idea. Paragraphs are separated by blank lines.
I-beam is another name for the insertion point. However, it is a less common term
An end mark is a punctuation mark that signals the end of a sentence or paragraph. Examples of end marks include periods, question marks, and exclamation points

questions.text: Which action does the F12 key perform?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: In many programs, pressing the F12 key opens the "Save As" dialog box. This allows you to save a new copy of the file you are working on and choose a name and location for it.
While some programs might use a function key combination with F12 to open a file, F12 by itself is not typically used to open a dialog box
There is no standard function for closing dialog boxes using just the F12 key. Most programs use the Esc key or the close button in the dialog box itself to close it.
The F12 key is not commonly used to delete anything. Deleting files or content is usually done with the Delete key or a menu option

questions.text: What is the bar that shows information and messages at the bottom of the document windows?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: The status bar is the correct answer because it is a horizontal bar at the bottom of a window that displays information about the current state of the document or application. For example, in a word processing program, the status bar might show the cur(Full-featured Word Processors)rent page number, the position of the cursor, and whether caps lock is enabled
The scroll bar is a vertical or horizontal bar that allows you to move the viewport of a document or window
The title bar is the bar at the top of a window that displays the name of the window
The toolbar is a bar that contains buttons and other controls that you can use to interact with a program

questions.text: To align the text in a paragraph to the Left margin in MS-Word document, which keyboard shortcut is used to change the case?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: CTRL+L is the keyboard shortcut for aligning text to the left margin in MS Word
ALT+L, Shift +L, and ALT +Shift +L are not used for changing the case or aligning text to the left margin in MS Word.

questions.text: c(A Simple Word Processor(Text Editor))
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Insertion mode is the most common mode used in word processors. In this mode, when you type a character, it is inserted at the cursor location, pushing any existing characters to the right. This is the default mode in most word processors and is the most intuitive way to type text

Overtype mode is a less common mode that is not available in all word processors. In this mode, when you type a character, it replaces the character at the cursor location. This mode can be useful for quickly correcting typos, but it can also be easy to accidentally overwrite text that you don't want to change
Title bar is not a mode of typing. The title bar is the bar at the top of a window that displays the name of the program and the document

Data entry mode is not a standard term used in word processing. It is possible that some word processors may have a mode called "data entry mode" that has specific features for entering data, but this is not a common term

questions.text: What is the bar that shows information and messages at the bottom of the document windows?

options.explanation: it is a horizontal bar at the bottom of a window that displays information about the current state of the document or application. For example, in a word processing program, the status bar might show the current page number, the position of the cursor, and whether caps lock is enabled
The scroll bar is a vertical or horizontal bar that allows you to move the viewport of a document or window
The title bar is the bar at the top of a window that displays the name of the window
The toolbar is a bar that contains buttons and other controls that you can use to interact with a program

options.explanation: 65 is the maximum number of columns you can insert in a Word document. If you try to enter more than 63 columns, Word will display an error message

options.explanation: Hidden text is a formatting option that applies to paragraphs, not characters. When you hide text, it is still there in the document but it won't be shown unless you unhide it

options.explanation: CTRL+H is a shortcut specifically for opening the Replace tab within the Find and Replace dialog box, allowing you to search for text and replace it with something else

questions.text: A tab stop is a position in the document usually measured from
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Tab stops are measured from left to right in a document. This means that when you press the Tab key, your cursor jumps to the next tab stop position along the left margin

options.explanation: An indent refers to the space between the text and the left or right margin of a paragraph. It creates a visual separation between paragraphs and helps improve readability.

questions.text: Which menu does the page setup option present in?

questions.text: The feature of word processor in which text automatically moves to the next line?

questions.text: Which of the following options does MS Word provide to manipulate graphics?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: MS Word can manipulate bitmap graphics. Bitmap graphics are images made up of pixels. Each pixel has a specific location and color. This allows for the creation of detailed and realistic images. However, bitmap graphics can lose quality when resized.
This is not a commonly used term for image formats. It is likely a misspelling of "bitmap graphic".

MS Word can also manipulate vector graphics. Vector graphics are images made up of paths and strokes. These paths and strokes are defined by mathematical equations. This allows vector graphics to be resized without losing quality.
